title: Finish letters
html:
TL;DR

  • Aug 25 2019, 1:59pm Apple Note from 17-year-old Yahir in GP: a three-line to-do — "Write email to teachers / Read / Work on garage plan" — title "Finish letters". Pre-senior-year-of-high-school logistics burst.
  • Created the same day as the first dad death premonition (Aug 25 2019, 10:22pm — ~8hrs later). The juxtaposition — banal afternoon checklist → midnight premonition — is striking; dropped here as a graph adjacency.
  • "Garage plan" — likely the Gtanos truck/garage workspace Yahir's father uses for his daily-work hauling. First mention in corpus of a "garage plan" project — could be teen-Yahir trying to organize the family workspace, or a personal build-out plan. Not enough signal yet to stub.
  • "Write email to teachers" — pre-senior-year (HS class of 2020) outreach; sibling to later college admissions / renaissance scholarship correspondence. The concept of the letter as form is already operative.
  • Graph value: dates a letters-as-output mode at age 17, pairs against dad-premonition timing, gestures at a "garage plan" sub-thread worth watching for recurrence.

Source quote

"Write email to teachers

Read

Work on garage plan"

Open questions

  • Garage plan — recur in the 2019–2020 corpus? If yes, stub it as a project. If no, junk-drawer.
  • Which teachers, and about what? Likely senior-year scheduling / college-rec letters — the form-not-content question.
  • Is the same-day adjacency to the premonition meaningful or coincidence? At minimum, the morning-Yahir / midnight-Yahir split-state of Aug 25 2019 is a documented contrast.

Wikilinks
